title: malleola woodfordii
intro: Malleola woodfordii, also known as the Woodford's Malleola, is a type of orchid native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The roots and leaves of Malleola woodfordii are used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as relieving bronchitis and asthma symptoms. In herbal preparations, Malleola woodfordii is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be used to treat various health conditions.
